name: Cell Cycle (G1–S–G2–M)
description: Cell cycle: cyclin-CDK complexes drive progression through G1, S, G2 and M, with the G1/S restriction point, the G2/M DNA-integrity checkpoint and the spindle-assembly checkpoint arresting the cycle for repair when conditions are not met.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["G1 phase"]
    N2["Cyclin D-CDK4/6"]
    N3{"Restriction point passed?"}
    N4["S: DNA replication"]
    N5["G2 phase"]
    N6{"G2/M: DNA intact?"}
    N7["Cyclin B-CDK1 -> mitosis"]
    N8{"Spindle attached?"}
    N9["Anaphase: sisters separate"]
    N10["Cytokinesis -> two cells"]
    N11["Arrest / repair"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|no| N11
    N3 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|damage| N11
    N6 -->|intact| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|not attached| N7
    N8 -->|attached| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 -->|next cycle| N1
    N11 -->|after repair| N1

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

sources.title: Cyclin-dependent kinases: engines, clocks, and microprocessors
sources.journal: Annual Review of Cell and Developmental Biology

sources.title: Cell cycle, CDKs and cancer: a changing paradigm
Checkpoints: controls that ensure the order of cell cycle events
sources.authors: Hartwell, L. H.; Weinert, T. A.
